content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 fix-now issues: Nearby-work bead IDs are still rejected by the review/final_review classification pipeline, and sibling/scope derivation depends on optional `br list` fields with no guaranteed fallback.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The new `Nearby work` IDs cannot actually be used for `planned-elsewhere` routing. Review-stage guidance is still built from `extract_pe_bead_ids` only (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195`), the classifier prompt still says `planned-elsewhere` only applies to beads listed under `Already Planned Elsewhere` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:417-425`), and `final_review.rs` strips any `mapped_to_bead_id` that is not in that PE-only allowlist before voting and again before restart routing (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1111-1123`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1844-1858`). When a prompt has Nearby work but no PE entries, every mapping to a sibling/dependent/related bead is discarded, so this bead's core end-to-end behavior still does not work.
   Rationale: This bead exists to let reviewers classify surrounding bead-owned work as `planned-elsewhere`. The current review/final_review pipeline still rejects those mappings unless the target bead was already copied into the separate PE section, which defeats the feature in the common case.
2. finding_summary: Sibling inference and one-line scope summaries currently depend on `br list` returning fields that the load path does not guarantee. `load_bead_summaries` only loads `br list --all --deferred --limit=0 --json` (`src/cli/project.rs:1837-1852`), but `nearby_sibling_entries` reads `summary.dependencies` to recover the parent epic (`src/cli/project.rs:2492-2522`, `src/cli/project.rs:2579-2585`) and nearby entry rendering reads `summary.description` for the scope line (`src/cli/project.rs:2473-2480`, `src/cli/project.rs:2611-2617`). Those fields are optional/defaulted on `BeadSummary` (`src/adapters/br_models.rs:253-267`), so a summary-shaped list response silently yields no siblings and title-only scope summaries, with no fallback.
   Rationale: The unit tests hand-populate rich summaries, but the production builder only has whatever `br list` provides. If that response omits parent/description data, two of the four nearby-work categories degrade or disappear, which misses the bead's acceptance criteria.
